title: Audit and fix contact form email address (sv.couverture95@gmail.com) in all mailto links and form fields

requirements.text: Audit every location in the codebase where a contact email address appears — including the Contact section form action/mailto link, the contact info card email display link, the Footer email link, and any other mailto: href — and verify that each one uses exactly 'sv.couverture95@gmail.com'. Correct any mismatched, missing, or malformed email addresses so that all mailto: links and form submission targets point to sv.couverture95@gmail.com.

quotes: Je ne reçois pas je me suis fait une demande a moi même je ne reçois rien peut tu regarder que cest la bonne adresse email sv.couverture95@gmail.com

acceptanceCriteria: Every mailto: href in Contact.tsx, Footer.tsx, and any other component uses exactly 'mailto:sv.couverture95@gmail.com' with no typos, extra spaces, or alternate addresses.
The contact form 'Envoyer' / submit button or form action opens a mailto: link pre-filled with sv.couverture95@gmail.com as the recipient.
The email displayed as text/link in the Contact section info card shows 'sv.couverture95@gmail.com' and its href is 'mailto:sv.couverture95@gmail.com'.
The email displayed as text/link in the Footer shows 'sv.couverture95@gmail.com' and its href is 'mailto:sv.couverture95@gmail.com'.
No other email address appears anywhere in the site components.
A test click on any email link opens a mail client with sv.couverture95@gmail.com as the pre-filled recipient.

constraints: Do not change any existing SEO meta tags, JSON-LD structured data, sitemap, or robots.txt.
Do not alter any content other than email address values and their associated mailto: hrefs.
Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
nonGoals: Implementing a server-side email sending backend — the site uses mailto: links only.
Adding new form fields or changing the form layout.
Any SEO, performance, or scroll-related changes.
